Lionel Mpasi.bio: A young goalkeeper developing in the Belgian Pro League with Sint-Truiden, Mpasi has emerged as a promising option between the posts for DR Congo. His shot-stopping ability and growing confidence have earned him the trust of the coaching staff.
Lionel Mpasi.playingStyle: Young, athletic goalkeeper with good reflexes, improving command of the penalty area, and the agility to make spectacular saves when called upon
Lionel Mpasi.worldCup2026Role: Starting goalkeeper whose development and shot-stopping ability provide the foundation for DR Congo's defense at their historic World Cup return
Lionel Mpasi.notableFact: Has developed his game in the competitive Belgian Pro League, gaining the experience needed for DR Congo's historic World Cup campaign

Chancel Mbemba.bio: The captain and defensive leader of DR Congo, Mbemba is a vastly experienced center-back who has played at Newcastle United, Porto, and Marseille. His leadership, aerial ability, and big-game temperament make him the cornerstone of the team's defense. Was briefly subject to a political detention in DR Congo which has since been resolved; fully available for the World Cup.
Chancel Mbemba.playingStyle: Experienced, commanding center-back with excellent aerial ability, strong tackling, and the leadership qualities to organize and inspire the defensive line
Chancel Mbemba.worldCup2026Role: Captain and defensive leader whose experience at Porto, Newcastle, and Marseille provides the spine of DR Congo's backline at their historic first World Cup in 52 years
Chancel Mbemba.notableFact: Won the Portuguese league title with Porto and has Champions League experience, making him by far the most decorated player in the DR Congo squad. Political detention resolved ahead of World Cup.

Arthur Masuaku.bio: A left-back with Premier League experience from his time at West Ham United, Masuaku brings technical ability and attacking flair to the left side of defense. His dribbling skills from fullback are a unique weapon in DR Congo's arsenal.
Arthur Masuaku.playingStyle: Technical, attack-minded left-back with excellent dribbling ability, good crossing, and the flair to create attacking opportunities from deep positions
Arthur Masuaku.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back whose attacking flair and Premier League experience provide width and creativity from the defensive line
Arthur Masuaku.notableFact: Spent several seasons at West Ham United in the Premier League, bringing top-flight English football experience to the DR Congo squad

Christian Luyindama.bio: A powerful, aggressive center-back who has built his career at Galatasaray in the Turkish Super Lig, Luyindama is known for his physical dominance and uncompromising defending. His warrior spirit embodies the fighting character of the DR Congo team.
Christian Luyindama.playingStyle: Physical, aggressive center-back who dominates aerial duels, makes crunching tackles, and brings an intimidating presence to the heart of the defense
Christian Luyindama.worldCup2026Role: Experienced center-back whose physical power and aggression provide a formidable defensive partnership alongside captain Mbemba
Christian Luyindama.notableFact: Has been a fan favorite at Galatasaray for his warrior-like defending and passionate commitment on the pitch

Ngonda Muzinga.bio: A homegrown center-back from AS Vita Club in the Congolese league, Muzinga represents the domestic talent that has contributed to DR Congo's historic World Cup qualification. His journey from local football to the world stage is an inspiring story.
Ngonda Muzinga.playingStyle: Solid, dependable center-back with good aerial ability, strong in the tackle, and the determination that comes from fighting through the competitive African club football landscape
Ngonda Muzinga.worldCup2026Role: Depth option at center-back who represents the domestic league contingent and whose journey from local football to the World Cup embodies the team's remarkable story
Ngonda Muzinga.notableFact: One of the few domestically-based players in the World Cup squad, representing the pride of Congolese club football on the world stage

Samuel Moutoussamy.bio: A disciplined defensive midfielder who has developed his craft in Italian football at Lecce in Serie A, Moutoussamy brings tactical intelligence and ball-winning ability to the DR Congo midfield. His Italian football education shows in his positional discipline.
Samuel Moutoussamy.playingStyle: Disciplined defensive midfielder with good positional sense, reliable tackling, and the tactical awareness developed through Italian Serie A football
Samuel Moutoussamy.worldCup2026Role: Midfield anchor whose tactical discipline and defensive awareness provide protection for the backline and a platform for the team's more creative players
Samuel Moutoussamy.notableFact: Has developed his game in Serie A's demanding tactical environment at Lecce, bringing Italian defensive discipline to the DR Congo midfield

Yoane Wissa.bio: One of the most exciting attackers in the Premier League, Wissa has been a revelation at Brentford with his pace, finishing, and ability to score spectacular goals. Returned fully fit after an AFCON injury absence. His explosive talent makes him DR Congo's most dangerous offensive weapon at their historic World Cup return — the nation's first in 52 years.
Yoane Wissa.playingStyle: Quick, dynamic striker with electric pace, clinical finishing, and the ability to score from anywhere with either foot, capable of producing moments of individual brilliance
Yoane Wissa.worldCup2026Role: Leading attacker and main goal threat whose Premier League form and explosive ability make him the player most likely to produce match-winning moments for DR Congo at their first World Cup since 1974
Yoane Wissa.notableFact: Has scored numerous spectacular goals in the Premier League for Brentford, establishing himself as one of the most exciting African strikers in English football. Returned from AFCON injury absence and is fully fit for the World Cup.

Axel Tuanzebe.bio: A versatile defender who came through Manchester United's academy and has played across the Premier League and Serie A. Tuanzebe became a national hero when he scored the extra-time goal that sealed DR Congo's historic World Cup qualification against Jamaica in the intercontinental playoff.
Axel Tuanzebe.playingStyle: Versatile, physically strong defender comfortable playing center-back or right-back, with good recovery pace and aerial ability from his Premier League development
Axel Tuanzebe.worldCup2026Role: Defensive option whose historic qualifying goal has made him a talisman for the squad; capable of playing center-back or fullback
Axel Tuanzebe.notableFact: Scored the extra-time winner against Jamaica on 2026-03-31 to send DR Congo to their first World Cup in 52 years, instantly becoming one of the most iconic figures in Congolese football history

Théo Bongonda.bio: A tricky winger with explosive pace and dribbling ability, Bongonda has played across Belgian and Spanish football. His directness and willingness to take on defenders make him a constant threat from wide positions.
Théo Bongonda.playingStyle: Pacey, direct winger who loves to run at defenders, with good dribbling skills, a dangerous left foot when cutting inside, and the speed to get behind defensive lines
Théo Bongonda.worldCup2026Role: Wide attacking option whose pace and directness provide an alternative threat from the flanks, capable of stretching defenses and creating space for teammates
Théo Bongonda.notableFact: Was a key player at Racing Genk in Belgium before moving to Spain, showcasing his exciting attacking talent across multiple European leagues

Cédric Bakambu.bio: A veteran striker who has scored goals across La Liga, the Chinese Super League, and Turkey with Galatasaray, Bakambu is DR Congo's most experienced forward. His movement, finishing, and understanding of the striker's role make him a reliable goalscorer.
Cédric Bakambu.playingStyle: Intelligent striker with sharp movement in the box, clinical finishing, and the experience to find space and score goals in the tightest of situations
Cédric Bakambu.worldCup2026Role: Experienced striker whose goal-scoring pedigree and big-game experience provide a reliable alternative or partner to Wissa in the forward line
Cédric Bakambu.notableFact: Became the most expensive African player in history when he moved to Beijing Guoan in 2018, having scored prolifically at Villarreal in La Liga

Gaël Kakuta.bio: A gifted playmaker who was once the subject of a famous transfer battle involving Chelsea as a teenager, Kakuta has journeyed through multiple European leagues. His creative ability, vision, and technical skill remain his greatest assets.
Gaël Kakuta.playingStyle: Creative, technically gifted playmaker with excellent vision, silky ball control, and the ability to thread passes through the tightest defensive lines
Gaël Kakuta.worldCup2026Role: Experienced creative option whose vision and technical ability provide a different dimension in attack, ideal for unlocking stubborn defenses
Gaël Kakuta.notableFact: Was the subject of one of football's most controversial transfers when he moved to Chelsea as a teenager, and has since built a well-traveled career across Europe

Silas Katompa Mvumpa.bio: An explosive winger known simply as Silas, he has dazzled in the Bundesliga with VfB Stuttgart. His electrifying pace, fearless dribbling, and ability to create chances from nothing make him one of the most exciting wide players in German football.
Silas Katompa Mvumpa.playingStyle: Lightning-quick winger with devastating pace, direct dribbling, and the ability to beat defenders on either side, creating constant danger from the left flank
keyStrengths: Ability to create chances from nothing
Silas Katompa Mvumpa.worldCup2026Role: Exciting wide attacker whose pace and unpredictability can turn any match in DR Congo's favor, a constant threat on the counter-attack
Silas Katompa Mvumpa.notableFact: Has been one of the standout wingers in the Bundesliga at VfB Stuttgart, known for his electric pace and crowd-pleasing style of play

Joël Kiassumbua.bio: A goalkeeper from TP Mazembe in the domestic Congolese league, Kiassumbua represents the homegrown talent in the squad. His experience in African club competition and the Congolese league provides a different perspective to the goalkeeping group.
Joël Kiassumbua.playingStyle: Experienced domestic goalkeeper with solid shot-stopping, good reflexes, and the familiarity with African football that provides valuable squad depth
Joël Kiassumbua.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper providing depth from the domestic league, his inclusion represents the pride of Congolese club football at the World Cup
Joël Kiassumbua.notableFact: Plays for TP Mazembe, one of the most storied clubs in African football history, five-time African Champions League winners

Meschack Elia.bio: A quick, direct winger who has impressed at BSC Young Boys in the Swiss Super League, Elia has contributed to multiple league titles. His pace on the counter-attack and ability to stretch defenses provide a useful tactical weapon.
Meschack Elia.playingStyle: Pacey winger with good dribbling ability, excellent at running in behind defenses, and the directness to take on defenders and create goal-scoring opportunities
Meschack Elia.worldCup2026Role: Wide attacking option whose pace and counter-attacking ability provide a valuable tactical weapon, particularly against stronger opponents who push forward
Meschack Elia.notableFact: Has won multiple Swiss Super League titles with BSC Young Boys and played in the Champions League group stages, gaining valuable European experience

Merveille Bokadi.bio: An experienced center-back from Standard Liege in the Belgian Pro League, Bokadi provides seasoned defensive depth to the squad. His years of professional football in Belgium have made him a dependable option in the heart of defense.
Merveille Bokadi.playingStyle: Experienced, reliable center-back with good aerial ability, strong marking, and the organizational skills developed through years of professional football in Belgium
Merveille Bokadi.worldCup2026Role: Experienced center-back providing defensive depth and the professionalism needed to maintain squad standards throughout the tournament
Merveille Bokadi.notableFact: Has been a loyal servant at Standard Liege in Belgium, one of the country's most historic clubs, gaining years of European football experience

Edo Kayembe.bio: A dynamic central midfielder who has built his career in English football at Watford, Kayembe brings energy, physical presence, and competitive spirit to the DR Congo midfield. His box-to-box running and tackling ability are his main strengths.
Edo Kayembe.playingStyle: Energetic box-to-box midfielder with good physical presence, strong tackling, and the stamina to cover ground and influence matches at both ends of the pitch
Edo Kayembe.worldCup2026Role: Dynamic midfield option whose energy and physicality provide competition and depth in the center of the park
Edo Kayembe.notableFact: Has been a consistent performer at Watford in the English Championship, bringing competitive English football experience to the DR Congo squad

Fabrice Ngoma.bio: A left-back from TP Mazembe in the domestic league, Ngoma is one of the homegrown players whose journey from Congolese football to the World Cup represents the incredible story of this DR Congo squad. His defensive commitment and local knowledge are valued by the team.
Fabrice Ngoma.playingStyle: Committed, hard-working left-back with good defensive positioning, determination in the tackle, and the spirit of a player who has earned his place through the domestic league system
Fabrice Ngoma.worldCup2026Role: Backup left-back option from the domestic league, his presence in the squad represents the contribution of Congolese club football to this historic campaign
Fabrice Ngoma.notableFact: Plays for TP Mazembe, the legendary Congolese club that has won the African Champions League five times and is one of the continent's most successful teams

Chadrac Akolo.bio: A versatile wide player who has built his career in French and German football, playing at Lens in Ligue 1 after spells in the Bundesliga. Akolo's pace, work rate, and ability to play across the front line make him a useful squad member.
Chadrac Akolo.playingStyle: Quick, hardworking wide attacker who presses from the front, provides width, and uses his pace to stretch defenses and create space for teammates
Chadrac Akolo.worldCup2026Role: Versatile attacking option who can fill multiple roles in the forward line, providing energy and pace from the bench when the team needs fresh legs
Chadrac Akolo.notableFact: Has played in both the Bundesliga and Ligue 1, bringing a well-rounded European football education to the DR Congo attacking options

Dieumerci Ndongala.bio: An experienced winger who has played across Belgian and Turkish football, Ndongala brings pace, trickery, and years of professional experience to the squad. His ability to take on defenders and create chances remains a useful asset.
Dieumerci Ndongala.playingStyle: Tricky, experienced winger with good pace, dribbling ability, and the know-how to create chances in wide areas after years of professional football across multiple leagues
Dieumerci Ndongala.worldCup2026Role: Experienced wide option providing squad depth and the ability to create chances with his dribbling and pace from the flanks
Dieumerci Ndongala.notableFact: Has built a long professional career across Belgium and Turkey, providing the kind of experienced squad depth that is essential in tournament football

Neeskens Kebano.bio: Named after the legendary Dutch midfielder Johan Neeskens, Kebano is a talented winger who has been a creative force at Fulham in the Premier League and Championship. His set-piece delivery and ability to create chances make him a valuable attacking weapon.
Neeskens Kebano.playingStyle: Creative winger with excellent set-piece delivery, good crossing ability, and the technical skill to produce moments of quality from wide areas or dead-ball situations
Neeskens Kebano.worldCup2026Role: Creative wide option whose set-piece expertise and crossing ability provide an important tactical weapon, particularly useful in tight tournament matches
Neeskens Kebano.notableFact: Named after Dutch legend Johan Neeskens, Kebano has lived up to his namesake's creative flair while playing in the Premier League with Fulham

Jackson Muleka.bio: A young striker with raw pace and finishing ability who has played in Turkish football, Muleka represents the next generation of DR Congo attacking talent. His speed and directness make him a dangerous option from the bench.
Jackson Muleka.playingStyle: Quick, direct striker with good finishing ability, raw pace to run in behind defenses, and the youthful energy to make an impact as a substitute
Jackson Muleka.worldCup2026Role: Young striker option whose pace and directness provide an alternative attacking approach, ideal for exploiting space when introduced as a substitute
Jackson Muleka.notableFact: Impressed at TP Mazembe before moving to European football, representing the pipeline of talent from Congolese football's famous development system

Dylan Batubinsika.bio: A dependable center-back who has established himself at Saint-Etienne in Ligue 1, Batubinsika provides reliable defensive cover. His consistent performances in French football have earned him a regular place in the DR Congo setup.
Dylan Batubinsika.playingStyle: Solid, consistent center-back with good aerial ability, reliable tackling, and the organizational skills to maintain the defensive line's shape and discipline
Dylan Batubinsika.worldCup2026Role: Reliable center-back option providing depth and consistency in the defensive ranks, capable of stepping in without the team losing quality
Dylan Batubinsika.notableFact: Has been a steady performer at Saint-Etienne in Ligue 1, one of France's most historic clubs, earning regular recognition with the national team

Dimitri Bertaud.bio: A goalkeeper developing in Ligue 1 with Montpellier, Bertaud provides additional goalkeeping depth to the squad. His experience in French football and steady development make him a capable backup option.
Dimitri Bertaud.playingStyle: Competent goalkeeper with solid fundamentals, good shot-stopping ability, and the composure that comes from regular football in one of Europe's top leagues
Dimitri Bertaud.worldCup2026Role: Backup goalkeeper providing experienced depth from Ligue 1, capable of stepping in if needed during the tournament
Dimitri Bertaud.notableFact: Has earned his place in the squad through consistent performances at Montpellier in Ligue 1, providing reliable goalkeeping depth

Marcel Tisserand.bio: A vastly experienced center-back who has played across multiple European leagues including the Bundesliga with Wolfsburg and the Turkish Super Lig with Fenerbahce, Tisserand is one of the most seasoned defenders in the squad.
Marcel Tisserand.playingStyle: Experienced, physical center-back with good aerial ability, strong marking, and the composure that comes from years of playing at a high level across Europe
Marcel Tisserand.worldCup2026Role: Experienced defensive option whose vast European experience provides invaluable depth and mentorship for younger defenders in the squad
Marcel Tisserand.notableFact: Has played in the Bundesliga, Turkish Super Lig, and Belgian football, giving him one of the most diverse footballing CVs in the DR Congo squad

Nathan Mangala.bio: A dynamic young midfielder who has earned a move to Nottingham Forest in the Premier League, Mangala brings energy, technical quality, and modern midfield attributes to the DR Congo engine room. His development has been rapid and impressive.
Nathan Mangala.playingStyle: Dynamic, box-to-box midfielder with good technical ability, sharp passing, and the energy to influence matches at both ends of the pitch with his tireless running
Nathan Mangala.worldCup2026Role: Emerging midfield talent whose energy and technical quality make him an increasingly important part of DR Congo's midfield, with the potential to be a standout performer
Nathan Mangala.notableFact: Earned a move to Nottingham Forest in the Premier League, reflecting his rapid development as one of the most promising young Congolese midfielders
